[2026-05-19] Claude - DONE: Fixed ref group not collapsing — template had IsContentSelectedInDocument=true on every track. Changed: Source/als_patcher.py
[2026-05-19] Claude - DONE: Rewrote insert_group_track() to match Ableton 12.4's canonical GroupTrack structure (extracted by diffing Sam's manually-saved collapsed group). Old template had AudioSequencer wrapper inside FreezeSequencer (old format), missing Mixer/PanMode, SoloSink, CrossFadeState, ViewStateSessionTrackWidth, and FreezeSequencer modulation targets. Malformed structure caused Ableton to ignore TrackUnfolded=false and open the group expanded. Now generated from a single _GROUP_TRACK_TEMPLATE string with 22 unique IDs allocated. Both projects rebuilt. Changed: Source/als_patcher.py
[2026-05-19] Claude - PARTIAL: Ref group still opens expanded in Ableton 12.4 despite matching canonical structure exactly. [2026-05-19] Claude - DONE: Built BPM detector (detect_bpm.py prototype) — finds kick onsets via windowed peak detection, computes median interval, normalises to 90-180 dance range. Tested on three real tracks: Coldabank ✓ (134 matched filename), Fire In Your Eyes (130, no filename hint), Lucozade (125, filename said 128 — slight 10ms window resolution gap). Successfully built three new test projects (Jones - Fire In Your Eyes, Coldabank - Never Say Sorry, Pressure - Lucozade). Detector not yet integrated into project_builder pipeline.

[2026-06-25] Claude - DONE: Flat-ref bounce track shipped. bounce.py sums mix stems in 1s lockstep chunks -> 32-bit float WAV (synthetic sum exact; 14 stems @ 2min in ~32s; peak preserved, no clip). als_patcher: added set_track_output_external + set_track_muted, removed GroupTrack insertion for refs. project_builder: REF_TRACK_COLOR=37, prints flat bounce of mix stems as last track, keeps supplied references as match tracks. [2026-06-25] Claude - DONE: Two features. (A) numpy acceleration for bounce.py — numpy fast path (~15x: 1.9s vs 29s on 14 stems) with pure-stdlib fallback; outputs bit-identical (maxdiff 0.0), so tool stays install-free. (B) Working-track grouping — drums/music/vocals/fx with 2+ stems wrapped in a GroupTrack (audible, Main, expanded, category-coloured); children route AudioOut/GroupTrack + TrackGroupId. [2026-06-25] Claude - DONE: Classifier hardening + audio-content analysis (after 5-pack test exposed full mixes polluting the flat ref + a kick named "K" missed). (1) Filename patterns: TEST MIX/ROUGH MIX/SCRATCH MIX -> reference; K/KCK -> kick. (2) New Source/stem_analysis.py (numpy FFT, no librosa/ffmpeg): per-band energy + crest + sustain. audio_label() flags full mixes (crest<=5 + 6+ bands + sustain>=0.6) — tuned against labelled real files, cleanly separates every real full mix (Ref Bounce/TEST MIX/SW Flat Mix/Far Away Master+Current) from all stems. Audio kick-detection dropped (kick vs bass overlap). (3) project_builder runs audio_label on music/unclassified suspects and moves full mixes to references (kept out of the flat-ref sum); numpy-optional, filename-only fallback. Reused the ANALYSIS APPROACH from Loudness Leveller (band energy) + Automated DJ Mixes (low-end), numpy-only. Changed: Source/stem_analysis.py (new), Source/stem_classifier.py, Source/project_builder.py, Documentation/AI_CONTEXT.md.
[2026-06-25] Claude - DONE: Group-bus / sub-mix detection (Sam: big frequent problem on real packs — a group bus left among the stems double-counts, "everything sounds wrong on play"). Added find_group_buses() to Source/stem_analysis.py: stems are sample-aligned so a bus == sum of members; non-negative greedy matching pursuit in Gram-matrix space flags any stem reconstructed >88% by a positive sum of >=2 others (non-negativity flags the bus, not its members). project_builder pulls detected buses out of the working layout + flat-ref sum and parks them at the very bottom (below refs), colour 37 grey (BUS_TRACK_COLOR), muted, Main out — recall by unmuting. patch_project handles category 'bus' (mute, no group, no lane-height). VALIDATED: caught Coldabank 'All Drums' + Far Away 'BASS EGYBE' (=b+b_3, Sam's exact case), no false positives on individual stems. Changed: Source/stem_analysis.py, Source/project_builder.py, Source/als_patcher.py, Documentation/AI_CONTEXT.md.
[2026-06-25] Claude - STARTED: Multi-version stem-pack handling (Sam hit it on Fallon: extended stems in Audio/ + radio edit in Audio/Edit STems subfolder, got mixed together). Captured full package-shape taxonomy in AI_CONTEXT (versions / category-subfolders / wet-dry / buses / full-mixes). Built Source/versions.py::detect_versions() — a subfolder whose element keys (after last _) mirror the top-level stems (>=50%) is an alternate VERSION; else a CATEGORY subfolder (flatten). VALIDATED: Fallon -> Extended(40)+Edit STems(39), 39/39 elements paired; Coldabank/Daydream -> None (flat). Layout: Extended -> 16-bar gap -> Radio -> other versions, shared element tracks, flat-mix per version. Still to build: layout engine (clip offsets on shared tracks), same-folder name-token versions (Get Right S16/S17), wet/dry (wet on, dry grouped+muted under). Changed: Source/versions.py (new), Documentation/AI_CONTEXT.md.
[2026-06-25] Claude - DONE: Multi-version LAYOUT engine. als_patcher: _build_clip_xml + insert_clip_into_track take base_start_beat offset and can append clips into an already-open Events block (stack versions on one track); patch_project places primary clip at its base_start_beat then extra_clips (later versions) on the same track, updating track end so appends land. project_builder: build_multiversion_project() — per-version classify/full-mix/bus/bounce, element pairing onto shared tracks, 16-bar-gapped offsets (VERSION_GAP_BARS), flat-ref bounce per version; build_project branches to it when detect_versions finds versions. VALIDATED single-version unchanged (Coldabank regress) + Fallon multi-version (Extended@33 + Edit STems@241, 36 tracks with stacked extended+radio clips, FLAT REF 2 clips, buses peach per version). Changed: Source/als_patcher.py, Source/project_builder.py, Documentation/AI_CONTEXT.md.
[2026-06-25] Claude - DONE: Fixed full-mix miss on Fallon's radio "Current" (a sum of the whole track summed into the flat-ref bounce). Root cause: un-mastered full bounces have crest ~9-10 (not <=5 like mastered masters), full spectrum + sustained, and aren't an exact stem sum — so they slipped both the crest<=5 full-mix rule and bus detection. Fix: (1) added \bcurrent\b to REFERENCE_PATTERNS (filename — reliable for these bounces); (2) added a second audio_label full-mix rule (active_bands>=9 AND active_frac>=0.7 AND crest<=15) for un-mastered full bounces. VALIDATED across Coldabank/Fallon(both versions)/Daydream: 7 files flagged as mix/master/bounce, ALL genuine, zero individual-stem false positives. Changed: Source/stem_classifier.py, Source/stem_analysis.py.
[2026-06-25] Claude - DONE: Multi-version grid-alignment + markers (Sam: radio edit kick off-grid because the file starts with a pre-downbeat swoosh, not beat 1). bpm_detector.detect_bpm now returns first_beat_sec (kick grid phase). build_multiversion: each version's base_offset = bar_line - (first_beat/60)*bpm so the KICK lands on a bar; all that version's stems shift together (stay in sync), swoosh becomes a pickup. Also added arrangement markers (als_patcher.insert_locators + patch_project locators param) at each version's downbeat, labelled Extended / Radio Edit / Dub / Instrumental (auto from filenames). Also: refs+buses now share tracks across versions (neatness). VALIDATED on Fallon: markers Extended@bar33 + Radio Edit@bar242 both exactly on grid; kick alignment guaranteed by construction. Changed: Source/bpm_detector.py, Source/als_patcher.py, Source/project_builder.py.

[2026-06-25] Claude - DONE: Multi-version neatness — refs + group buses now SHARE tracks across versions by element (extended clip + radio clip stacked on one row), like the working tracks, instead of separate per-version tracks. _shared() helper in build_multiversion_project. [2026-06-25] Claude - DONE: numpy-ified find_audio_regions RMS (was the pure-Python per-sample loop the 3.14.0 interpreter kept miscompiling -> persistent build crash on Fallon). VALIDATED region-equivalent to stdlib on 24-bit + 16-bit stems (identical regions -> tight trims unchanged). Bonus: much faster on big packs. Changed: Source/als_patcher.py.
